primary_written_source.citation: Cropley, Steve. "McLaren F1: Full Test." Autocar, May 11, 1994.
primary_written_source.chicago_bibliography: Cropley, Steve. "McLaren F1: Full Test." *Autocar*, May 11, 1994.
primary_written_source.notes: Tested at Bruntingthorpe and Millbrook. 0-60 in 3.2 sec, 230+ mph top speed. The full magazine scans of Car Magazine's companion June 1994 road test are included in magazine/.

artifact_types.period-artifact: Contemporaneous artifact from the primary source era — actual magazine page scans, factory press photographs, period advertising, celebrity-delivery news photos, auction-catalog documentation. Cite per its own provenance.
artifact_types.specimen-photograph: Modern photograph of the original vehicle, typically from a motor show, museum, or collection display. Supplements period artifacts by showing the physical car today.

image_primary_sources.creator: CNN / Motor1 archival footage
image_primary_sources.description: A 28-year-old Elon Musk taking delivery of his silver McLaren F1 in 1999 — bought for roughly $1 million after selling Zip2. The F1 was one of only 64 road cars built and Musk's first supercar.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: CNN archival footage, *Elon Musk Takes Delivery of His McLaren F1*, 1999, via InsideEVs, https://insideevs.com/features/465537/elon-musk-getting-delivery-mclaren-f1/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: CNN archival footage. *Elon Musk Takes Delivery of His McLaren F1*. Still photograph. 1999. Via InsideEVs. https://insideevs.com/features/465537/elon-musk-getting-delivery-mclaren-f1/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: CNN archival footage, *Elon Musk Takes Delivery of His McLaren F1*, 1999.

image_primary_sources.creator: CNN / Motor1 archival footage
image_primary_sources.description: Another still from the 1999 delivery footage — Musk watching the F1 being unloaded from the enclosed transporter.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: CNN archival footage, *Elon Musk Receives McLaren F1*, 1999, via InsideEVs, https://insideevs.com/features/465537/elon-musk-getting-delivery-mclaren-f1/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: CNN archival footage. *Elon Musk Receives McLaren F1*. Still photograph. 1999. Via InsideEVs. https://insideevs.com/features/465537/elon-musk-getting-delivery-mclaren-f1/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: CNN archival footage, *Elon Musk Receives McLaren F1*, 1999.

image_primary_sources.creator: CNN / Motor1 archival footage
image_primary_sources.description: Musk standing beside the delivered F1 — the young dot-com millionaire beside the car that, a year later, he would crash on Sand Hill Road while giving Peter Thiel a ride.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: CNN archival footage, *Elon Musk Standing Beside His New McLaren F1*, 1999, via InsideEVs, https://insideevs.com/features/465537/elon-musk-getting-delivery-mclaren-f1/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: CNN archival footage. *Elon Musk Standing Beside His New McLaren F1*. Still photograph. 1999. Via InsideEVs. https://insideevs.com/features/465537/elon-musk-getting-delivery-mclaren-f1/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: CNN archival footage, *Elon Musk Standing Beside His New McLaren F1*, 1999.

image_primary_sources.wikimedia_title: File:Gordon Murray chats in the paddock behind the pits at the 1996 Le Mans.jpg
image_primary_sources.creator: Martin Lee
image_primary_sources.description: Gordon Murray — the F1's designer — in the paddock at Le Mans, one year after his F1 GTR won the 24 Hours overall.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Martin Lee, *Gordon Murray in the Paddock at the 1996 Le Mans*, 1996, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 2.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gordon_Murray_chats_in_the_paddock_behind_the_pits_at_the_1996_Le_Mans_(51816281021).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Lee, Martin. *Gordon Murray in the Paddock at the 1996 Le Mans*. Photograph. 1996. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 2.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gordon_Murray_chats_in_the_paddock_behind_the_pits_at_the_1996_Le_Mans_(51816281021).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Lee, *Gordon Murray in the Paddock at the 1996 Le Mans*.

image_primary_sources.creator: Car Magazine (Bauer Media)
image_primary_sources.license: Fair use, educational — magazine cover scan
image_primary_sources.description: The June 1994 cover of Car Magazine featuring the McLaren F1 supercar test — a contemporaneous peer publication to Steve Cropley's Autocar 11 May 1994 test.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: *Car Magazine*, June 1994 cover, McLaren F1 supercar test, https://www.carmagazine.co.uk/car-reviews/magazine-reviews/the-first-mclaren-f1-review-car-magazine-june-1994-supercar-test/.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: "McLaren F1 Review: Our Original 1994 Road Test." *Car Magazine*, June 1994. Cover image via carmagazine.co.uk. https://www.carmagazine.co.uk/car-reviews/magazine-reviews/the-first-mclaren-f1-review-car-magazine-june-1994-supercar-test/.

image_primary_sources.wikimedia_title: File:1995 Le Mans winning McLaren F1 GTR display at Goodwood Festival of Speed.jpg
image_primary_sources.creator: Spycatcher58
image_primary_sources.description: The McLaren F1 GTR that won the 1995 24 Hours of Le Mans outright — the racing validation of the road car Cropley tested the year before.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Spycatcher58, *1995 Le Mans-Winning McLaren F1 GTR at Goodwood Festival of Speed*, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1995_Le_Mans_winning_McLaren_F1_GTR_display_at_Goodwood_Festival_of_Speed.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Spycatcher58. *1995 Le Mans-Winning McLaren F1 GTR at Goodwood Festival of Speed*. Photograph. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 4.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1995_Le_Mans_winning_McLaren_F1_GTR_display_at_Goodwood_Festival_of_Speed.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Spycatcher58, *1995 Le Mans-Winning McLaren F1 GTR*.

image_primary_sources.wikimedia_title: File:Mclaren F1 GTR - John Nielsen & Thomas Bscher at Donington 1995.jpg
image_primary_sources.creator: Martin Lee
image_primary_sources.description: The Nielsen/Bscher McLaren F1 GTR at the 1995 Donington BPR round — in-period competition photography of the racing F1.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Martin Lee, *McLaren F1 GTR, Nielsen and Bscher at Donington, 1995*, 1995, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 2.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mclaren_F1_GTR_-_John_Nielsen_%26_Thomas_Bscher_at_Donington_1995_(49672809008).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Lee, Martin. *McLaren F1 GTR, Nielsen and Bscher at Donington, 1995*. Photograph. 1995. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 2.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mclaren_F1_GTR_-_John_Nielsen_%26_Thomas_Bscher_at_Donington_1995_(49672809008).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Lee, *McLaren F1 GTR, Nielsen and Bscher at Donington, 1995*.

image_primary_sources.creator: MrWalkr
image_primary_sources.description: A 1994 production McLaren F1 — the model year Cropley tested at Bruntingthorpe and on the Yorkshire Dales drive for Autocar's 11 May 1994 cover story.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: MrWalkr, *1994 McLaren F1*, 2026, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1994_McLaren_F1_R26.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: MrWalkr. *1994 McLaren F1*. Photograph. 2026. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 4.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1994_McLaren_F1_R26.jpg.

image_primary_sources.creator: Calreyn88
image_primary_sources.description: A second angle of the XP4 prototype — the development mule that validated the carbon-fiber monocoque Murray's team built from scratch.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Calreyn88, *1993 McLaren F1 XP4 Prototype, Rear Three-Quarter*, 2022, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1993_McLaren_F1_XP4_(15513).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Calreyn88. *1993 McLaren F1 XP4 Prototype, Rear Three-Quarter*. Photograph. 2022. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 4.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1993_McLaren_F1_XP4_(15513).jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Calreyn88, *1993 McLaren F1 XP4 Prototype, Rear Three-Quarter*.

image_primary_sources.creator: Calreyn88
image_primary_sources.description: XP4 — one of five McLaren F1 prototypes built between 1992 and 1993 at McLaren Cars' Woking facility, used for the press demonstrations that led to Steve Cropley's May 1994 Autocar road test.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Calreyn88, *1993 McLaren F1 XP4 Prototype*, 2022, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1993_McLaren_F1_XP4.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Calreyn88. *1993 McLaren F1 XP4 Prototype*. Photograph. 2022. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 4.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1993_McLaren_F1_XP4.jpg.

image_primary_sources.wikimedia_title: File:1996 McLaren F1 Chassis No 63 6.1 Front End.jpg
image_primary_sources.creator: Chelsea Jay
image_primary_sources.description: Detail of F1 chassis 063 front end, showing the under-bumper crash structure and aerodynamic splitter characteristic of Murray's production design.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Chelsea Jay, *1996 McLaren F1 Chassis No. 63 Front End*, 2019, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1996_McLaren_F1_Chassis_No_63_6.1_Front_End.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Jay, Chelsea. *1996 McLaren F1 Chassis No. 63 Front End*. Photograph. 2019. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 4.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1996_McLaren_F1_Chassis_No_63_6.1_Front_End.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Jay, *1996 McLaren F1 Chassis No. 63 Front End*.

image_primary_sources.creator: Chelsea Jay
image_primary_sources.description: The BMW S70/2 V12 engine bay of a McLaren F1 LM — the gold-foil heat-shield lining Gordon Murray specified for thermal reflectivity is clearly visible.
image_primary_sources.chicago_caption: Chelsea Jay, *1995 McLaren F1 LM Engine Bay*, 2018, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1995_McLaren_F1_LM_6.1_Engine.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_bibliography: Jay, Chelsea. *1995 McLaren F1 LM Engine Bay*. Photograph. 2018. Wikimedia Commons. CC BY-SA 4.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1995_McLaren_F1_LM_6.1_Engine.jpg.
image_primary_sources.chicago_footnote_short: Jay, *1995 McLaren F1 LM Engine Bay*.

notes_on_use: Period artifacts (magazine scans, delivery photos, press kits) are used under fair-use educational exemption for this UTK museum exhibit.
Specimen photographs are Wikimedia Commons CC / PD images — attribution per the license strings.
All chicago_caption / chicago_bibliography strings are pre-formatted.
When rendering captions, link to source_page (not source_url) so the viewer lands on the article that provides provenance.
